articles.title: Con Edison to spend $29B shoring up NYC area grid as electrification rises
articles.summary: Consolidated Edison plans to invest over $29 billion by 2030 to upgrade its New York City and suburban grid infrastructure, primarily driven by building and transportation electrification rather than data centers. This significant investment, detailed in a first-quarter earnings presentation, aims to meet accelerating demand, address growing reliability concerns, and support New York's clean energy initiatives, including the addition of 22 new substations by 2034.
articles.content: Consolidated Edison plans to invest over $29 billion by 2030 to upgrade its New York City and suburban grid infrastructure, primarily driven by building and transportation electrification rather than data centers. This significant investment, detailed in a first-quarter earnings presentation, aims to meet accelerating demand, address growing reliability concerns, and support New York's clean energy initiatives, including the addition of 22 new substations by 2034.

The company's core business model continues to focus on IT distribution and services for various sectors, with future earnings growth anticipated from cloud and modernization services.

articles.title: West Pharma (WST) Faces Cybersecurity Attack, Stock Drops 3.6%
articles.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services (WST) experienced a 3.6% stock price decline after reporting a cybersecurity breach on May 4, 2026, where unauthorized parties accessed and encrypted data. Despite the setback, GuruFocus identifies WST as 13.2% undervalued with a strong GF Score of 96/100, suggesting potential for investors. The company is actively recovering from the incident, with core systems back online for some operations, but the full financial impact is still being assessed.

articles.title: State Street holds 5.8M Williams‑Sonoma shares (NYSE: WSM) — 4.9% stake
articles.summary: State Street Corporation has reported a beneficial ownership of 5,797,853 shares of Williams-Sonoma Inc (NYSE: WSM), representing a 4.9% stake in the company as of March 31, 2026. The filing, a Schedule 13G, indicates shared voting power of 3,606,030 shares and shared dispositive power of 5,794,102 shares through its affiliated investment-adviser subsidiaries. This disclosure clarifies the attribution of holdings across State Street's adviser affiliates and indicates a passive investment approach.

articles.title: SanDisk and Micron Fall 9%, Western Digital Drops 8% as Memory Supercycle Trade Hits Pause Button
articles.summary: Shares of SanDisk, Micron, and Western Digital fell by 8-9% in coordinated trading, signaling profit-taking after significant parabolic gains rather than a fundamental shift in the memory supercycle. Despite the pullback, strong Q3 earnings from all three companies, along with continued tight DRAM and NAND supply and hyperscaler AI infrastructure spending, continue to support a long-term bull case. The article notes that such single-day swings are characteristic of the volatile memory sector and that multi-day consolidations are normal after substantial advances.
articles.content: Shares of SanDisk, Micron, and Western Digital fell by 8-9% in coordinated trading, signaling profit-taking after significant parabolic gains rather than a fundamental shift in the memory supercycle. Despite the pullback, strong Q3 earnings from all three companies, along with continued tight DRAM and NAND supply and hyperscaler AI infrastructure spending, continue to support a long-term bull case. The article notes that such single-day swings are characteristic of the volatile memory sector and that multi-day consolidations are normal after substantial advances.

articles.title: News Corp (Class A) stock (US65249B1098): Media giant's steady presence in digital shift
articles.summary: News Corp (Class A) maintains a strong position in the media landscape through its diverse portfolio, including news, book publishing, and digital real estate services. The company emphasizes quality journalism and data-driven insights, with subscriptions, advertising, and real estate services as key revenue drivers. Its dual-class share structure and Nasdaq listing offer US investors exposure to a media powerhouse adapting to digital content consumption trends.
articles.content: News Corp (Class A) maintains a strong position in the media landscape through its diverse portfolio, including news, book publishing, and digital real estate services. articles.title: ResMed stock (US7611521078): CEO exercises options amid NYSE price drop
articles.summary: ResMed CEO Mick Farrell exercised stock options and sold shares under a 10b5-1 plan as the NYSE-listed stock dropped 3.7% to $199.12. The company, a leader in sleep apnea and respiratory care devices, maintains a Buy rating from analysts with an A$48.00 target. ResMed continues to lead in the market with robust product lines and digital innovation, offering stability for investors.

articles.title: Google AI legend raises big money for startup targeting this cutting-edge goal
articles.summary: Demis Hassabis is the co-founder and CEO of Google’s DeepMind artificial-intelligence lab, which has spun out a startup called Isomorphic Labs to use AI to accelerate the creation of new drugs.
articles.content: Demis Hassabis is the co-founder and CEO of Google’s DeepMind artificial-intelligence lab, which has spun out a startup called Isomorphic Labs to use AI to accelerate the creation of new drugs.
